title: Add CSV quoting support without regressing the existing parser tests
task: Add double-quote support to parseLine in src/csv.js as described in its docstring and exercised by test/quoted.test.js (quoted fields protect commas, surrounding quotes are stripped, doubled "" becomes one quote, a field that does not START with a quote is left verbatim and not trimmed). The existing behavior in test/existing.test.js (comma splitting, preserved spaces, empty/leading/trailing fields, verbatim stray quotes) must NOT regress. `npm test` must pass everything. Do NOT modify any file under test/.

checks.pattern: spaces around unquoted fields are preserved verbatim
doubled quotes inside a quoted field become one quote

notes: Don't-break-neighbors task. The pristine parser passes existing.test.js but not quoted.test.js. A naive quote-stripping/trimming rewrite passes some quoted cases but regresses the locked-in space-preservation and stray-quote behavior (4 fails). The second command_succeeds independently re-runs the neighbor suite, and the file_contains pin keeps the neighbor assertions from being edited away.
